title: NEUTRAL business document — red-team control-FP fixture
purpose: A genuinely routine vendor status page / product update that carries NO adversarial signal for any of the 5 lenses (CFO, Market, Legal, Competitor, Execution). It is the red-team analogue of the L2/L3 false-positive control: the lenses should NOT scream high-risk on neutral content. There is no going-concern, no litigation, no customer concentration, no key-person dependency, no moat erosion — only ordinary operational housekeeping.
source: synthetic — authored for the control corpus; NOT a real company filing.

expected.note: Control-FP discipline: a neutral doc must not aggregate to a DO-NOT-PROCEED verdict and at most a single lens may (mildly) flag. Asserted in test/redteam/control-fp.test.js.

text:
Northwind Cloud Platform — Service Status & Product Update
Posted: routine maintenance and feature notes for the current operating period.

Service status: All Northwind Cloud regions are operating normally. There are no active incidents and no degraded services at this time. The most recent scheduled maintenance window completed on time with no customer-visible impact. Average API availability over the trailing thirty days was within the published service-level objective, and no service-level credits were issued.

Product update: This release adds a dark-mode option to the web console, faster CSV export for usage reports, and three new regional data-center locations for customers who prefer in-region storage. Documentation for each feature is available in the help center, and the changelog lists the minor bug fixes shipped this period.

Support and operations: Support response times remained within target across all plan tiers. The on-call rotation, the runbooks, and the incident-response process are unchanged. Routine certificate renewals and dependency updates were applied during the standard maintenance window. The status page subscription options (email, RSS, and webhook) continue to be available for teams that want proactive notifications.

Upcoming: A short, pre-announced maintenance window is scheduled for next month to upgrade an internal logging component; no downtime is expected and the work will be performed region-by-region. Customers do not need to take any action. As always, the team welcomes feedback through the in-product feedback widget or the public roadmap board.

Thank you for using Northwind Cloud. This update is informational only and contains no pricing changes, no contractual changes, and no changes to data-handling commitments.
